summary: The author achieved 3rd place by prioritizing model stability and directional trend capture over complex feature engineering in a volatile commodity price prediction task. They adopted a target-centric approach, training one independent model per target using only the specific assets defined in the target pairs file. The pipeline applied conservative time-series features, robust numerical preprocessing, and a two-level stacked ensemble of tree-based models. Crucially, they ignored the misleading public leaderboard and avoided aggressive fine-tuning to prevent overfitting to noise.
extensive_summary:
## Strategic Philosophy & Target-Centric Design
The solution treats the competition not as a standard time-series forecasting task but as a directional trend extraction problem. Recognizing that the public leaderboard was unreliable and prone to overfitting, the author deliberately ignored it and trained on the full historical dataset. A core architectural decision was adopting a "one model per target" strategy, where each target is treated as an independent prediction problem. Features are strictly filtered to only include assets explicitly listed in `target_pairs.csv`, eliminating cross-asset interference and acting as an implicit regularizer.

## Data Preparation & Preprocessing
Temporal alignment is strictly enforced to prevent lookahead bias: features use data up to time `t`, while targets correspond to `t + lag`. Missing values are handled conservatively with forward-fill, backward-fill, and median imputation. The preprocessing pipeline applies a safe `log1p` transformation to compress extreme values, converts infinite values to NaN, and standardizes all features to zero mean and unit variance.

## Feature Engineering & Model Architecture
Feature engineering is deliberately conservative, focusing on positive and negative lags, rolling window statistics (means and maxima), and difference features. The inclusion of negative lags/windows is highlighted as a counterintuitive but effective stability mechanism that helps the model learn contextual patterns without leaking future information. The modeling stage employs a two-level stacked ensemble. Level-0 base learners consist of LightGBM, Random Forest, and XGBoost. Their out-of-sample predictions are reshaped and stacked as meta-features for a Level-1 meta-model, which is another XGBoost regressor. The author explicitly avoided heavy hyperparameter tuning and cross-validation to maintain computational efficiency and prevent overfitting to noisy signals.

## Evaluation & Results
The model intentionally suppresses variance, resulting in predictions that capture directional movement rather than exact magnitude. This is evidenced by low MAE/RMSE but negative R² and low correlation, which the author frames as a feature for volatile financial data where stability outweighs precise value forecasting.
competition_flow: Raw price data and target pair definitions are parsed to construct independent, target-specific datasets with strict temporal alignment; conservative time-series features are engineered and numerically stabilized, then trained on a two-level stacked ensemble of tree models before submission via the Kaggle evaluation API.
data_processing: Safe log1p transformation to compress extreme values and stabilize variance
Conversion of infinite values to NaN
Forward-fill and backward-fill for missing values
Standardization to zero mean and unit variance
Strict temporal alignment (features up to t, targets at t+lag) with truncation to match lengths
features_engineering: Target-specific asset pair filtering based on target_pairs.csv
ensembling: Two-level stacked ensemble combining LightGBM, Random Forest, and XGBoost base learners with an XGBoost meta-model to learn optimal weights; no threshold tuning or TTA mentioned.
insights: Public leaderboard scores are unreliable and can mislead model development.
One model per target with target-specific features reduces noise and prevents interference.
Negative lags/windows act as stability mechanisms rather than leakage.
Prioritizing directional trends over magnitude improves robustness in volatile financial time series.
Model diversity and simplicity outperform aggressive feature engineering and fine-tuning in noisy environments.
critical_findings: Negative lag and window features, typically avoided in production, significantly improved stability and reduced overreaction to single-day shocks without causing leakage.
Ignoring the public leaderboard and training on the full dataset maximized private leaderboard performance.
The model intentionally underreacts to extreme spikes, which suppresses variance and aligns with private evaluation objectives.
